item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
that last paragraph is so sad. dust dude wants a dust pal. it doesn't want to be alone anymore. but whatever makes dust dude what they are, they can't magic that into any of their creations. but they can't bear to reuse the dust because to them, that's a friend lost, even if it never lived. sad. :'( wish i could give dust dude a hug.

also, to me it was a little reminiscent of space crab making that painting of their home, then knocking it off the easel and retreating to a corner to cry (essentially). I've decided to make it canon that they sometimes get to hang out together and feel less alone. since space crab needs to wear an isolation suit anyway, maybe it could work. DON'T TELLL ME OTHERWISE! :'(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This does it for me.

When creating a humanoid, SCP-2583 typically spends triple the time creating it as it would spend on creating another structure of comparable size. Once it has completed a humanoid, it will remain within 10 centimeters of it for between 20 and 30 hours, after which it will negate the electrostatic charges keeping the humanoid together. SCP-2583 separates the remains of these humanoid structures and does not reuse the dust. To date, none of the humanoids created by SCP-2583 have displayed independent mobility.

The dustman attempts to create a friend, and failed. And it is greatly disturbed by this. Life must be horribly lonely for this fellow. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your interpretation of the sentence is correct & I appreciate you bringing the confusion to my attention. I'll be making a correction momentarily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel strange about the 1st sentence of the last paragraph. Perhaps it's a grammar mistake?

When creating a humanoid, SCP-2583 typically spends triple the time creating it as it would another structure of comparable size.

The structure 'it would' seems lack of something. Maybe the following sentence will be better?

When creating a humanoid, SCP-2583 typically spends triple the time creating it as it would spend on another structure of comparable size.

In fact, this sentence causes some kind of misunderstanding. Our CN tranlator seems can't get the exact meaning. Some of CN members pointed out this strange translation, and I find myself can't understand this structure either. I'm not sure if this is a mistake. If someone can explain that to me, I will really appreciate it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright then. This article reminds me a lot of SCP-1352 in that both involve living dust creatures. It wouldn't be too far out of line for the Foundation to look into whether or not the two phenomena are variations of one another, or if SCP-1352 was created by 2583 or visa versa. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2583 is a mobile, sentient structure made of dust. Its baseline form masses 1.7 grams and measures 3.7 centimeters in height. Its form is somewhat indistinct due to its composition, but is roughly humanoid, with four strands of dust representing limbs extending from a central strand which terminates in a spheroid made of dust.

To this:

SCP-2583 is a mobile, sentient structure made of dust. Its baseline form masses 1.7 grams and measures 3.7 centimeters in height. Its form is somewhat indistinct due to its composition, but is roughly humanoid, with four strands of dust representing limbs extending from a central strand which terminates in a spheroid made of dust.1

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Similarity between SCP-2583 and SCP-1352 has been noted. Investigation into whether the two phenomena are variations of the same effect, or if one propagated the other, is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great SCP, definitely a +1.
My only question is - why is this a Euclid? I mean, the containment procedures have to be pretty stringent but besides that this thing is harmless. I don't see a reason it would require Euclid ranking unless there's something else you're not telling us…
*dramatic music*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Original, concise, to the point and kind of …poetic. I like it! Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this is just okay. It's a cute little things what does stuff. Certainly has a unique composition, but the iffy tone drove me to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edited the "is" to "it"…

after which is will negate the electrostatic charges keeping the humanoid together

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kinda-sorta. IIRC, that was a stone carved into a face that sculpted sand for no discernible reason without an explanation for how, whereas this is a dust man who physically manipulates the dust to make things and apparently has a reason.

Yes, everything he makes, he makes by "hand". He just changes the electrostatic properties to act like "glue" to hold it all together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this a pseudo-rewrite of that old Series 1 skip that got deleted? I approve.
